text: This is Dr. Craig Rogers, Director of Renal Surgery at the Vatacuda Urology Institute at Henry Ford Hospital.

The robotic instrument elevates the kidney, allowing the fourth arm prograsp instrument to remove the robotic bulldog clamps from the renal hilum. The prograsp instrument grasps the notch of the clamps, enabling the console surgeon to sequentially remove the clamp from the renal vein followed by the clamp on the renal artery.

After unclamping the renal hilum, the surgeon places additional sutures to close the renal defect. Hemlock clips are cinched to secure the closure, and laparotide clips may be used to prevent sliding. If bleeding is observed, further sutures and cinching of hemlock clips are applied.

In conclusion, robotic partial nephrectomy is a safe and feasible procedure in selected cases even with multiple tumors. Use of the fourth robotic arm especially in obese patients or patients with multiple tumors facilitates the procedure and gives a greater degree of autonomy to the surgeon.
